preamble: The shift from [a] to [oi] is a complex diphthongization that often involves an intermediate palatal stage or the influence of a following palatal consonant.

examples.note: Evolution from Latin tonic [a] in certain environments through [ei] to [oi] stages.
Historical development in certain morphological contexts or through contraction.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
